name: Universal Civic Floor requires targeted participation gate
summary: UCF must be a targeted floor with participation requirements, not unconditional support, to prevent population inflation and predator adaptation.

name: UCF activates automatically on diversity threshold
summary: Floor should activate when participation diversity drops below threshold and deactivate when recovered, functioning as pulses not constant subsidies.

name: Penumbra Accord maintains systemic diversity
summary: Penumbra serves dual function: individual mediation zone AND systemic maintenance of minimum participation diversity during founding phases.

name: Minimum-compliance gamers are transitional scaffolding
summary: GAMER participants peak during system fragility and fade when stability established, indicating healthy structural behavior not failure mode.

name: Civic Tome enables emergent collective anticipation
summary: Collective governance anticipation emerges from pattern memory, clear thresholds, and time for rhythms to stabilize without requiring dedicated predictive infrastructure.

name: Civic Tome optimized for pattern retrieval
summary: Civic Tome's amendment history functions as anticipation mechanism, should be optimized for pattern retrieval not just archival.

name: Token decay must scale with participant adaptation
summary: Astris decay rate must scale at most as fast as participant adaptation capacity; complexity scaling faster than adaptation causes collapse.

name: Token system includes complexity-load monitor
summary: Token system should monitor governance complexity vs participant adaptation ratio and pause new demands when threshold exceeded.

name: Structural diversity required at system founding
summary: Diversity mechanisms must be present at founding, not introduced in response to crisis; late diversity is fragile after adaptation forms.

name: Pilot onboarding requires multiple participation modes
summary: Constella pilots must include multiple participation modes from day one; founding window is pre-stress period.

name: Strategic retreat enables system survival
summary: Under sustained pressure, temporarily dissolving contested mechanisms allows adversarial specialization to decay and effectiveness to restore.

name: Penumbra includes formal dissolution pathway
summary: Penumbra Accord crisis protocols should include formal dissolution pathway for contested mechanisms.

name: Three interlocking mechanisms required for stability
summary: No single mechanism produces stability; founding diversity, targeted floor, and tolerance of minimum-compliance must work together.

name: Any two mechanisms without third produces lesser outcome
summary: Any two mechanisms without the third produces collapse or degraded stability; all three together produced only long-run stability.
